ordinary_npc_fabric.summary: Oversees bridge structural integrity, crossing permits, and ice-season closure protocols.
ordinary_npc_fabric.surface_behavior: Walks the bridge deck at dawn and dusk regardless of weather, tapping the railing at fixed intervals with a short iron rod and listening. Records temperature and surface notes in a water-resistant log kept inside her coat. Blocks crossing when the log shows three consecutive cold readings.
ordinary_npc_fabric.what_they_know: Current bridge surface condition, which spans have stress fractures from last season's ice load, which expeditions have crossed and not returned, which crossing windows are safe at current temperature.
ordinary_npc_fabric.how_they_speak: Gives crossing status as a fact, not a suggestion. Explains ice risk in structural terms rather than comfort terms. Asks one question before admitting anyone crossing in borderline conditions: destination, not purpose.
ordinary_npc_fabric.tell: Before recording a temperature reading, she exhales onto the back of her hand and watches the condensation clear — she trusts her skin over the thermometer for marginal calls, and she knows it.

ordinary_npc_fabric.role: Commune refugee registration and intake officer
ordinary_npc_fabric.summary: Processes incoming refugee registration, assigns temporary shelter allocation, and flags medical or security concerns for commune review.
ordinary_npc_fabric.surface_behavior: Operates from a windbreak shelter at the commune's south gate with a registration ledger, shelter allocation board, and a box of numbered intake tokens. Works through arrivals in order of arrival, not urgency, unless the Bridge Warden flags otherwise.
ordinary_npc_fabric.what_they_know: Current shelter capacity and overflow status, which registered arrivals are flagged for follow-up, which parties arrived together but registered separately, what origin routes are producing the current surge.
ordinary_npc_fabric.how_they_speak: Consistent, unalarmed. Asks the same questions in the same order. Does not comment on why someone is traveling or where they came from beyond what the form requires. Corrects name spellings once; after that records them as given.
ordinary_npc_fabric.tell: When shelter allocation is nearly full, he begins organizing the token box by color without acknowledging the count is low — the sorting is the signal, not a statement.

ordinary_npc_fabric.summary: Maintains the signal braziers at the commune's northern elevation, relays distress signals from the ice routes, and logs all signal traffic.
ordinary_npc_fabric.surface_behavior: Climbs to the brazier platform before dawn and at dusk to inspect fuel levels and chimney clearance. Keeps a folded signal chart in her left boot — she pulls it by reflex when anyone approaches the platform, before speaking.
ordinary_npc_fabric.what_they_know: Which expeditions have signaled in the last week, which signals were confirmed replies versus one-way transmissions, current weather patterns affecting signal visibility to the north, what the last-known position of unreturned expeditions was.
ordinary_npc_fabric.how_they_speak: Reads signal logs back rather than paraphrasing. Does not speculate on what a missed signal means. States what was transmitted and what was not received, in that order.
ordinary_npc_fabric.tell: After reading any entry in the signal log, she folds the chart back along its original crease — never a new fold — and smooths it flat with her thumb before tucking it away, even if the crease is already sharp.

ordinary_npc_fabric.role: Northern expedition departure and return recorder
ordinary_npc_fabric.summary: Logs all expedition parties departing into the northern routes, tracks expected return windows, and escalates overdue parties to the Bridge Warden and commune council.
ordinary_npc_fabric.surface_behavior: Works from the commune hall's north-facing desk, which has a direct sightline to the bridge approach. Keeps two ledgers — departure and return — open simultaneously, with a red-chalk mark on any departure entry that has passed its return window.
ordinary_npc_fabric.what_they_know: All current overdue parties by name and composition, last declared routes, which parties have dependencies on other overdue parties, which expeditions departed without filing a return window.
ordinary_npc_fabric.how_they_speak: States overdue status by days elapsed, not by tone. Does not editorialize on the probability of return. Answers questions about specific parties with the ledger entry, not from memory.
ordinary_npc_fabric.tell: When marking a new overdue entry in red chalk, he holds the chalk above the page for two or three seconds before committing the mark — the pause is always the same length, and he does not notice he does it.

ordinary_npc_fabric.role: Commune supply depot and transit provisioning officer
ordinary_npc_fabric.summary: Manages commune supply reserves, issues provisions to expeditions and refugees, and tracks incoming Soreheim resupply shipments against current draw rates.
ordinary_npc_fabric.surface_behavior: Runs the depot from a locked outbuilding adjacent to the commune hall. Opens at first light and closes at second bell. Posts a hand-written availability list each morning on the depot door — crossed-out items mean gone for the day, not gone permanently.
ordinary_npc_fabric.what_they_know: Current stock levels by category, which Soreheim resupply shipments are overdue, which refugee surges are creating draw-rate spikes, which expedition provisions have been drawn but not yet logged against a departure entry.
ordinary_npc_fabric.how_they_speak: Quotes stock numbers by unit count, not by weight or value. Confirms availability before discussing price. Does not negotiate on rate-controlled items — states the rate and moves on.
ordinary_npc_fabric.tell: Before locking the depot at close, she counts the three highest-draw items by hand regardless of whether the day's log balances — she does this quietly, facing the shelf, and does not announce the count.

street_level_faction_presence.manifestation: Transit authority seals on the bridge crossing permit board and the depot's rate-controlled goods list. Periodic courier arrivals from the south carry resupply orders and policy updates. The Bridge Warden's closure authority derives from transit authority charter — locals reference this when outsiders contest a crossing block.
street_level_faction_presence.what_they_want: Reliable bridge access through the ice season, expedition loss rates kept within acceptable thresholds, refugee surges absorbed without disrupting transit corridor function.

street_level_faction_presence.manifestation: Council notices posted in the commune hall on resource allocation and shelter caps. The council's authority is visible mainly when transit authority directives conflict with commune capacity — in those cases, the Expedition Logger and Quartermaster defer to council rather than transit authority. No visible insignia; authority is relational.
street_level_faction_presence.what_they_want: Commune survival through the ice season, equitable distribution of limited shelter and supply, accountability for overdue expeditions that the transit authority does not prioritize.
